_about: Tunable parameters behind the computed (astronomical) layer. Edit here — no code changes needed. Every value was calibrated against the pinned Jantri years 549, 557, 558 (36/36 sangrands; see test/).
_ayanamsaJ2000: Sidereal offset (deg) at J2000, Lahiri/Chitrapaksha-type. Any value in 23.85–23.96 reproduces all 36 pinned sangrands; standard Lahiri sits inside the plateau.
_location: Sunrise reference for the day-assignment rules (sangrand day and udaya tithi).
_nsEpochGregorianOffset: Nanakshahi year N begins in March of Gregorian year N + 1468 (NS 1 = 1469 CE).

rules._sangrandDay: The solar month begins on the sunrise-to-sunrise day containing the sankranti; a sankranti after midnight but before sunrise belongs to the previous civil day. Forced by 11/36 pinned sangrands.
rules._tithiDay: Events fall on the day whose sunrise is inside the target tithi.
rules._kshaya: A tithi skipped between sunrises puts the event on the civil day the tithi actually runs (calibrated: purnmashi printed 2025-12-04).
rules._vridhi: A tithi present at two sunrises takes the first day (calibrated: Parkash Guru Hargobind 558).
rules._adhikMasa: A lunar month without a sankranti is adhik and never hosts events (matches the 558 adhik Jeth, printed as ਵਾ:ਜੇਠ).
